111147.explanation: It is always about the qualities of people, not about gender.

111148.explanation: There are sufficient opportunities for residents of Boxtel to give their opinion. Referenda usually cause much disappointment because the result is ultimately not respected.

111155.explanation: Employers should not be obligated to do this, but they should have the possibility to house workers on their own premises. Especially for seasonal work this relieves the existing housing market.

111157.explanation: Fireplaces and stoves can be used in a way that releases much less fine dust. This can be done by using clean wood, using a modern stove, and installing filters. BALANS finds it going too far for the government to interfere with these kinds of things.

111160.explanation: It must be prevented that bulky waste is dumped because it costs money to have it collected.

111162.explanation: People with disabilities must be able to fully participate in social activities.

111167.explanation: Boxtel already has a well-functioning system with music vouchers for children.

111168.explanation: Sports clubs must be financed by their members. If there are too few members, apparently there is less need for that sport. The municipality can help to get more members.

111170.explanation: More enforcement officers, supplemented by more street coaches who can help young people with problems.

111171.explanation: There is nothing wrong with camera surveillance. And we find the privacy argument less important than safety.

111172.explanation: More important is that housing for starters comes.

111173.explanation: The majority of social housing is already allocated to people from Boxtel.

111177.explanation: Active participation is important for everyone, including people with benefits.

111178.explanation: People who need help from the municipality must actually receive it.

111179.explanation: The standard in Boxtel is already higher than the national standard.

111181.explanation: The municipality should focus on 1 or 2 fast charging stations where electric cars can be charged quickly.

111183.explanation: No distinction should be made based on religion, culture, or belief. That would be discriminatory.

111184.explanation: It is not only about price but also about (proven) quality.

111185.explanation: The property tax (OZB) should remain as it is.

111188.explanation: Without entrepreneurs there is no economy and without economy no income for the government. So entrepreneurs must be able to continue doing business.

111190.explanation: Because there is too little living space, much more attention should be paid to creating (temporary) housing in the rural area.

111191.explanation: There should be no barriers to dropping off waste at the recycling center; handing in waste should actually be encouraged.

111196.explanation: The municipality does not decide on the curriculum of primary schools.

111200.explanation: Livability and social cohesion in neighborhoods is of great importance.

111203.explanation: Let the Market remain as it is for a few years. Changing it every year creates uncertainty, and the current system with more space for terraces in summer works fine!

111204.explanation: The municipality cannot force companies to leave; companies make their own choices and we must respect that. Of course companies must comply with laws and regulations.

111205.explanation: We work according to the principle, school follows child. So if a new neighborhood is built with (many) children, a primary school must be there.

111206.explanation: The municipality does not decide on the establishment of a supermarket. With the construction of a new residential area in Boxtel Northwest including the possibility for shops, there will be a supermarket near Esch.

111207.explanation: BALANS believes that the housing of labor migrants can be of much higher quality if it takes place in larger complexes. This allows for better management and supervision, resulting in improved housing quality. By doing so, more homes become available for people who live permanently in the Netherlands.

111208.explanation: This must be realized as soon as possible!

111147.explanation: Given equal suitability and availability, that is our preference. We have an experienced female candidate alderman.

111148.explanation: Involvement of residents in important matters is essential. That is why we support advisory referendums. The form this takes must suit the subject and the timing.

111155.explanation: Housing on agricultural business sites leads to cluttering of the rural area. Moreover, working for and also living near the employer encourages exploitation of labor migrants.

111157.explanation: Yes, provided the use does not cause nuisance. The municipality can promote conscious wood burning through information. Good stoves are preferred over open fireplaces. No burning during fog and similar conditions.

111160.explanation: We do not consider free bulky waste collection at home desirable or cost-effective. The focus should be on reuse, good separation, and cost-covering waste policy. The recycling center provides a good opportunity to drop off bulky waste.

111162.explanation: This is primarily the responsibility of entrepreneurs; the municipality can support this, for example through subsidies. For its own buildings, the municipality should organize accessibility as a role model.

111167.explanation: The municipality of Boxtel already offers a music voucher that provides children with financial support for music lessons. We find it important that this scheme continues and remains easily accessible.

111168.explanation: The municipality provides financial support to sports clubs; this support must be continued.

111170.explanation: The number of BOA's (special enforcement officers) has recently been expanded. Nevertheless, we continue to receive complaints about various nuisances, such as fat-bikers, parking on wheelchair-friendly paths and dog fouling.

111171.explanation: Camera surveillance can be expanded where needed, provided it is proportional, temporary, and respects privacy.

111172.explanation: A municipal starter loan is an effective tool to help first-time buyers on the housing market. In addition, we strive for the construction of more small, affordable homes. This helps starters structurally.

111173.explanation: Combinatie95 explicitly supports applying the option to allocate 50% of affordable rental and owner-occupied homes preferentially to house seekers from the own municipality.

111177.explanation: Participating in society is important, and socially meaningful work can contribute to that. This requires customization, voluntariness, and support, not a general obligation.

111178.explanation: The municipality already offers debt relief for residents with low incomes who cannot pay municipal taxes; that support instrument must be maintained.

111179.explanation: The existing debt relief scheme is an important safety net for residents with low incomes and helps to prevent debt problems. Currently, the income limit is set at 125% of the social assistance standard. We would like to see this limit raised to 130%.

111181.explanation: Provided that the number of charging spots grows proportionally with the number of electric cars. Good distribution across the municipality is important.

111183.explanation: If possible, certainly take account of religion and culture, but without hard guarantees. Especially considering the staff shortages in healthcare.

111184.explanation: In youth care, quality, continuity, and appropriate care must be paramount, with a reasonable price as a precondition.

111185.explanation: Lowering the property tax (OZB) puts municipal finances under pressure. A stable, trend-based development is wiser. We do not support increases beyond the trend level.

111188.explanation: Developments such as the Greentech Park Brabant are desirable, provided there is sufficient own power generation and storage. Also, small-scale expansion of Daasdonk in Liempde is welcome. We especially want to prioritize local companies that provide employment suitable for Boxtel.

111190.explanation: The municipality has a legal task to house status holders, but structural priority in social rental housing is not desirable. There must remain a fair distribution among other house seekers.

111191.explanation: Dropping off waste at the recycling center must remain free for recyclable products and waste for which a disposal contribution has already been paid.

111196.explanation: Although it is not a necessary municipal task, we value the preservation of the dialect. We consider the local dialect as intangible heritage. Of course, it is up to parents and children whether they want to speak dialect in daily life.

111200.explanation: The municipality must provide sufficient meeting places, not necessarily a separate community center in every neighborhood.

111203.explanation: A better green, car-free Market is cleaner, safer and more attractive to stay. Combinatie95 wants to retain parking spaces for the less able. It is important to combine the car-free market with good parking routes and signaling of available parking spaces.

111204.explanation: The nuisance caused by slaughterhouse Vion is large and requires strong municipal efforts to reduce noise, odor and pressure on the living environment. However, forcing a departure is not up to the municipality.

111205.explanation: In young neighborhoods, a primary school can be logical, but that does mean that other schools must shrink. This requires a careful consideration within the total educational offer. For Heem van Selis, a school in or near the neighborhood is a good solution.

111206.explanation: A supermarket in Esch would be desirable but the municipality cannot enforce this; feasibility lies with entrepreneurs. The new weekly market in Esch is a nice addition to the offerings.

111207.explanation: The housing of labor migrants in Vrilkhoven fits within the established policy and the published tender. The goal is decent housing and reducing nuisance in residential areas. Taking a different position now is administratively unreliable and can cause substantial damage claims.

111208.explanation: The Baroniestraat can be reopened after the double level crossing is closed. The current one-way measure in the Baroniestraat is temporary and we find it a fine measure that ensures a fairer distribution of traffic flows and more safety.

111147.explanation: CDA Boxtel aims for a college that can govern Boxtel best. As far as CDA is concerned, this is not dependent on gender. We support diverse governance.

111148.explanation: The municipal council is elected through a democratic process. One of the roles of the council is to represent the residents. We are in favor of participation by residents on important subjects and issues that directly affect their living environment.

111155.explanation: CDA Boxtel supports qualitative, good and humane housing in a small-scale way. We support the renewed quality marks, such as those established by LTO. We will continue the dialogue with the agricultural sector on this.

111157.explanation: This remains a choice for the residents. As a municipality, we have the responsibility to provide good information about the health effects of burning wood. For example, we can add the stove guide to a municipal app.

111160.explanation: We support the 'polluter pays' principle. Collecting bulky waste costs money. This must therefore be cost-covering.

111162.explanation: CDA Boxtel believes that all buildings must be accessible to everyone. It remains the responsibility of the building owner to realize this.

111167.explanation: Every child must have the opportunity to experience music lessons. In the municipality of Boxtel, we have various options for children whose parents do not have the financial means. We believe that everyone should be able to participate in society regardless of financial situation.

111168.explanation: Sports are important for connection and health. We contribute to this as a municipality by providing regular subsidies. If extra money is needed, the added value for the number of athletes will be considered.

111170.explanation: Safety on the streets is very important. Therefore, we support the deployment of enforcement officers (BOAs) to ensure safety and enforcement.

111171.explanation: Safety is very important. To improve safety and reduce nuisance, we support increased camera surveillance in relevant locations. The use of camera surveillance can contribute to extra security in situations of potential staff shortages.

111172.explanation: We support a starter loan. However, our preference is for homeownership starter constructions.

111173.explanation: We prefer that social rental homes are allocated to housing seekers from the local municipality.

111177.explanation: We assist residents receiving social assistance benefits in finding suitable, socially meaningful (volunteer) work, taking their personal situation into account.

111178.explanation: We continue to waive municipal taxes for financially vulnerable residents.

111179.explanation: We focus on securing livelihoods. We favor raising the threshold for all minimum income schemes to 130% of the social assistance standard. Additionally, we believe there should always be help available for people living in poverty.

111181.explanation: We do not support converting parking spaces into electric charging spots. We are exploring the possibilities for fast-charging stations on the outskirts of the village.

111183.explanation: People should have freedom of choice.

111184.explanation: Every child must receive good care. When selecting organizations, the focus should mainly be on quality. Price is also an important indicator.

111185.explanation: The property tax (OZB) should not increase more than the normal trend.

111188.explanation: In Boxtel we still have a lot of unused land with the right destination. We should first focus on the realization of projects like Vorst B.

111190.explanation: CDA Boxtel is not in favor. However, we follow national laws and regulations in this matter.

111196.explanation: We are proud of our cultural heritage. However, it is not the municipality’s task. Schools are responsible for their own curriculum.

111200.explanation: We strive for a neighborhood center/community house in every district.

111203.explanation: We aim for as lively a center as possible. That is why the market is car-free on weekends from spring through autumn. Outside this period, parking on the market is allowed.

111204.explanation: As a municipality, we must focus on enforcing regulations.

111205.explanation: A primary school is essential for a vibrant neighborhood and brings education closer to home. We see opportunities for a multifunctional building.

111206.explanation: As a municipality, we should facilitate this. We want amenities as close to home as possible. A supermarket benefits livability. Ultimately, it is up to the entrepreneur to realize this.

111207.explanation: We reject the plan for large-scale housing in Vrilkhoven. Social attitudes have changed and the proposal no longer aligns with the intended legislation. We choose solutions that suit Boxtel and support balanced municipal development.

111208.explanation: As far as the CDA is concerned, the one-way traffic solution is temporary until the double railway crossing is closed. Then a new plan must be made for traffic flow in this part of Boxtel, taking into account residents of Baroniestraat.

111147.explanation: The municipal government should be a good reflection of the people living in our municipality. With an equal number of men and women, that is. In such a government, all perspectives are represented to make fair decisions.

111148.explanation: In a representative system, citizens elect representatives to gather information, hear experts, and weigh various interests; this careful consideration is lacking in an individual yes/no vote in a referendum. However, we do support citizen assemblies.

111155.explanation: Those who bring people to our municipality to work must ensure these workers have decent housing without burdening our residents. There is no place for exploitation and nuisance in our municipality.

111157.explanation: In residential neighborhoods, the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should be discouraged because they can cause nuisance to people with respiratory problems.

111160.explanation: Garbage, old mattresses, and other litter on the street are annoying, unsafe, and unhealthy. The easiest way to prevent this is by collecting bulky waste for free. This avoids hassle and gives us cleaner streets.

111162.explanation: Accessibility is vital for people with disabilities. Many important places are not yet properly accessible, for example for someone in a wheelchair. Entrepreneurs often cannot afford such adjustments on their own. Therefore, we believe the municipality should provide assistance.

111167.explanation: Also explore options to provide music lessons at primary schools.

111168.explanation: (Sports) clubs are important for the wellbeing of our residents. Of course, financial contributions should be linked to spending objectives.

111170.explanation: We strive for more enforcement officers and neighborhood police officers; all residents must feel safe on the streets.

111171.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. Unfortunately, camera surveillance is necessary for this.

111172.explanation: We support all measures that increase opportunities for starters on the housing market.

111173.explanation: The housing shortage is enormous. We want to prevent children of residents from being forced to move to another municipality because they cannot find housing in our own municipality.

111177.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don’t want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions only create more uncertainty and cause new problems.

111178.explanation: If debt forgiveness of municipal taxes stops, people with the lowest incomes in our municipality will get into big trouble. They simply cannot miss that money. We believe everyone should be able to keep their head above water, which is why we support debt forgiveness.

111179.explanation: Absolutely. Too many people cannot make ends meet monthly and fall into isolation, avoid care, and choose an unhealthy, cheap lifestyle. Especially in cases of long-term poverty, the municipality should raise the income limit and provide more active support in employment, health, and wellbeing.

111181.explanation: We encourage electric driving, which must of course also be facilitated. Many neighborhoods lack the option to charge a car at their doorstep, so this must be enabled by the municipality.

111183.explanation: This is not a direct task of the municipality, but we do believe that care only works well if it aligns with someone's background, values, and beliefs. Care that fits the individual is a basic requirement for successful care.

111184.explanation: The waiting lists and problems in youth assistance and youth care are enormous. You don’t solve this by cutting more and always choosing the cheapest options. We must especially ensure certainty and provide help that matches the concerns of our youth.

111185.explanation: A reduction of the property tax (OZB) is not realistic at the moment. Boxtel faces major challenges that require investments, which cost money. We want to take responsibility for a balanced budget and prevent the municipality from running a deficit.

111188.explanation: We want to better utilize existing business locations before considering expansion.

111190.explanation: This is a national legal obligation.

111191.explanation: This is currently not free and should remain so. The polluter pays.

111196.explanation: We disagree. Education in the Brabants dialect is not a task for primary schools. Besides basic skills like language, reading, and arithmetic, attention to culture and society is very important.

111200.explanation: Instead, we want to provide more support to existing neighborhood centers.

111203.explanation: The residents / entrepreneurs are still very divided on this statement. There is currently an ongoing investigation, which we want to wait for.

111204.explanation: We want Vion to close before 2035. There is enormous animal suffering, and Vion causes a lot of odor and (water) pollution. The CO2 emissions are enormous. The heavy work is done by many labor migrants. They are treated very poorly, and housing is very problematic.

111205.explanation: This could also be a branch of an existing primary school.

111206.explanation: It is not a municipal task to provide this, but the municipality can stimulate it.

111208.explanation: One-way traffic is safer, but then the road must also be designed as such and not as it is now with only a provisional sign on the street.

111147.explanation: VVD Boxtel is neutral regarding the gender of aldermen.

111148.explanation: The municipal council must take responsibility as the representative of the residents of Boxtel.

111155.explanation: Employers who use cheap labor have a responsibility to limit nuisance.

111157.explanation: Many people use a pellet stove or open fireplace to (additionally) heat their home. The municipality cannot simply prohibit that.

111160.explanation: Everyone is responsible for their own waste. Free collection means the community pays for individual costs.

111162.explanation: Accessibility of buildings is important but not at the expense of the municipality.

111167.explanation: Music lessons are a valuable addition for children but not at the expense of the municipality. There are vouchers available for low-income individuals to take music lessons.

111168.explanation: Sports clubs are important institutions in the village. If they encounter financial problems but there is prospect for improvement, support from the municipality may be desirable.

111170.explanation: There is a lot of nuisance and insecurity in the center of Boxtel and elsewhere. Police deployment is limited. Therefore, additional municipal enforcement officers (BOA’s) are needed.

111171.explanation: There is a lot of nuisance and insecurity, for example in the center of Boxtel. In addition to BOA’s and police, camera surveillance is needed.

111172.explanation: The municipality should not offer financial products. That is for banks. It is the municipality’s task to simplify procedures for housing construction so more and faster building can take place.

111173.explanation: There is a shortage of (rental) housing. Therefore, space should first be provided for residents from the municipality itself.

111177.explanation: Quid pro quo. Moreover, socially meaningful work contributes to a social network and work rhythm, which makes the step towards regular employment smaller.

111178.explanation: Remission (waiver) is only intended for the very lowest incomes. In Boxtel, too many people use this arrangement for whom it is not intended.

111179.explanation: Support for poverty is only intended for the very lowest incomes. In Boxtel, too many people use this arrangement for whom it is not intended.

111181.explanation: There is a shortage of parking spaces. By allocating more spots for electric cars, the shortage becomes greater for everyone else.

111183.explanation: There is a shortage of personnel in healthcare and care costs are rising rapidly. These kinds of additional demands increase shortages and cause costs to rise further.

111184.explanation: Boxtel currently spends a lot on youth care compared to comparable municipalities. These costs must be lowered.

111185.explanation: There are many unnecessary expenses in Boxtel. These can be cut, and thus the property tax (OZB) can be reduced.

111188.explanation: More space for high-quality Boxtel entrepreneurs.

111190.explanation: There is a shortage in the housing market. Many people from Boxtel have been waiting a long time for a home. It is unfair if newcomers are prioritized over residents who have been waiting a long time.

111191.explanation: The environmental station currently asks a fair price. With separated delivery, much waste is already free. This should remain so.

111196.explanation: The Brabant dialect is passed on through the family to the children. The municipality does not need to interfere with this.

111200.explanation: Invest in existing neighborhood centers instead of more dispersion.

111203.explanation: During the terrace season, the market is largely car-free. Outside that period, a car-free market has no added value.

111204.explanation: VION must strictly comply with nuisance standards (odor and noise). If they can meet these, the company does not need to leave.

111205.explanation: The lack of a primary school in this new neighborhood is a missed opportunity because many young families live here.

111206.explanation: A supermarket can only exist if there is enough demand. The municipality should not open a supermarket itself. However, it can support local initiatives (such as the weekly market).

111207.explanation: Vrilkhoven is not a suitable location for large-scale housing of labor migrants.

111208.explanation: A northern ring road is a structural solution to Boxtel’s traffic problem.

111147.explanation: D66 considers it important that the government has proportional representation of our citizens.

111148.explanation: Involvement of residents and being able to participate is important to make good decisions. A referendum is one example. The most suitable way to participate differs per subject. We also want to further investigate a citizen’s assembly.

111155.explanation: Nuisance in residential neighborhoods must be prevented, but good supervision is often lacking in the rural area. Moreover, work and home must be separated. Therefore, depending on the situation, it is sometimes better not to house seasonal workers on the company premises.

111157.explanation: D66 does not support a complete ban on wood burning. Better information about the correct way to burn can greatly reduce nuisance and emissions. In addition, rules are needed about which weather conditions wood burning is prohibited.

111160.explanation: The polluter pays principle also applies to the collection of bulky waste. Collection and processing should be as cost-effective as possible, which means that payment will be required for bulky waste collection.

111162.explanation: Everyone should be able to participate in Boxtel. Entrepreneurs should therefore be encouraged to make their buildings accessible, for example with a loan or subsidy.

111167.explanation: Making music is important for development. D66 wants every child to have the opportunity to take music lessons.

111168.explanation: Financial support must be evaluated on a case-by-case basis. If there are no comparable sports clubs and the club has a solid future plan, support from the municipality is possible.

111170.explanation: Borough officers (BOA's) have taken over many tasks from the police. Extra efforts are needed to perform all these tasks properly.

111171.explanation: Camera surveillance is an intrusive measure that should be used sparingly and preferably temporarily, for example at high-risk events or in cases of persistent severe nuisance.

111172.explanation: A starter’s loan can make owning a home attainable for some first-time buyers. Therefore, a starter’s loan at favorable interest rates should be made possible.

111173.explanation: This sounds like a sympathetic measure but has many drawbacks. For example, Boxtel residents moving back from outside would no longer get a chance. Moreover, we want Boxtel residents to also have opportunities elsewhere.

111177.explanation: It is reasonable to ask for a small reciprocal contribution to society. We will need volunteers badly in the coming years. Naturally, this should be proportionate to one’s capacity.

111178.explanation: In certain situations, waiving local taxes can help people get out of debt and/or poverty. In the long term, this saves money and gives people the chance to build a better life.

111179.explanation: This limit was recently raised and has proven to be a very costly measure with limited effect. D66 favors targeted measures that achieve more with less money.

111181.explanation: It is important to establish good charging infrastructure to make electric driving attractive, even for those without a private driveway. However, the number of spots should gradually grow alongside electric car ownership, since these spots come at the expense of regular parking spaces.

111183.explanation: With current personnel shortages, we do not have the luxury to always meet this requirement. Of course, it is desirable to consider residents’ wishes where possible.

111184.explanation: It is never wise to focus solely on price; quality is also crucial, especially for a vulnerable group like our youth.

111185.explanation: The property tax (OZB) is around the national average and can remain so. In the coming years we face major challenges such as housing construction, energy transition, and quality education, which require significant investments.

111188.explanation: There is a great demand for business space in Boxtel. Greentech partly fulfills this, and there is still much room for infill, but expansion is also needed. We see opportunities to give the business park a better future with a solid area vision.

111190.explanation: We depend on national regulations and will therefore continue to prioritize urgent groups for rental housing.

111191.explanation: The polluter pays principle remains valid. Disposing of separated waste is usually free, but residual waste, rubble, and expensive materials to process do incur costs.

111196.explanation: It is up to schools themselves to determine the content of their curriculum.

111200.explanation: A meeting place fosters social cohesion in the neighborhood. In many areas this already works well, but in Northeast Boxtel such a place still lacks. It is important to explore whether functions can be combined for an affordable result.

111203.explanation: Parking in the city center must be much better organized. Better signage and ample parking spaces. Free parking further away, paid parking closer in. Parking is not needed on the Market square.

111204.explanation: Vion causes far more disadvantages than advantages to Boxtel. Labor migrants, odor, and traffic in an important central location in Boxtel. Vion’s departure offers many opportunities for the business park and housing construction in Boxtel South.

111205.explanation: Boxtel Northeast has virtually no facilities. Therefore, D66 supports relocating the Hobbendonken to Boxtel Northeast.

111206.explanation: It is up to entrepreneurs themselves to start a supermarket again in Esch. Unfortunately, that is not a municipal role. Fortunately, residents have taken initiative and there is once again a small weekly market.

111207.explanation: D66 has always opposed this plan. Four hundred labor migrants in the countryside where only 100 people live is asking for trouble. We need to limit the number of labor migrants.

111208.explanation: The current temporary measure causes too much nuisance for other residents. Due to the delay of PHS, the measure lasts too long and should be reversed pending the implementation of PHS.

statements.title: Boxtel must ensure that Esch gets a supermarket again.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves must remain permitted in Boxtel.

titleParts.text: In Boxtel moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The residents of Boxtel must be able to give their opinion on important issues through a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Boxtel moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
More municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on public safety must be deployed in Boxtel.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Boxtel must stop the construction of housing for labor migrants in Vrilkhoven.

titleParts.text: Boxtel moet de bouw van woningen voor

titleParts.glossary: People from another country who work temporarily in the Netherlands.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: More CCTV surveillance must be installed in Boxtel.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The market in Boxtel must be made car-free.
The municipality must make it possible for all primary schools to include attention to the Brabant dialect.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: The Baroniestraat must be reopened to two-way traffic.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Every neighborhood in Boxtel must receive its own community center.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
A primary school must be built in Heem van Selis.
In Boxtel, the business parks may expand.
Dropping off waste at the recycling centre should remain free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: Boxtel should pursue the departure of slaughterhouse Vion from the municipality.
